channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Judge rejects computer repairman's defamation claims over reports on Hunter Biden laptop
item.description: A Delaware judge has rejected defamation claims filed by a computer repairman against Hunter Biden, CNN, Politico and Joe Biden’s presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Randall Chase, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Daniel Day-Lewis ends acting retirement for a movie directed by his son
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Daniel Day-Lewis is coming out of retirement, seven years after his last movie, for a film directed by his son Ronan Day-Lewis. The project was announced Tuesday by Focus Features and Plan B, who are partnering on “Anemone.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The dockworker strike could impact Utahns — Here's how
item.description: Dockworkers at 36 ports across the eastern United States are on strike. Depending on the duration, this could lead to shortages and price hikes in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Helene's victims include first responders who died helping others
item.description: Falling trees and raging floods from Hurricane Helene killed more than 150 people when it struck Florida late last week and then plowed through the Southeast as one of the deadliest storms in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A chemical cloud moving around Atlanta's suburbs prompts a new shelter-in-place alert
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Residents east of Atlanta were again warned Tuesday to take shelter where they are if shifting winds push the still-billowing chemical cloud from a chlorine factory fire over their neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Kirk becomes a hurricane as North Carolina reels from Helene
item.description: Desperate residents of the storm-battered mountains of western North Carolina lined up for water and food, hunted for cellphone signals and slogged buckets from creeks to flush toilets days after Hurricane Helene’s remnants deluged the region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department finds Georgia is 'deliberately indifferent' to unchecked abuses at its prisons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia prison officials are “deliberately indifferent” to unchecked deadly violence, widespread drug use, extortion and sexual abuse at state lockups, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BY JEFF MARTIN, KEVIN McGILL and ALANNA DURKIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KTLA 5

item.title: Watch: JD Vance, Tim Walz to face off in 2024 vice presidential debate
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz will meet Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: New bill proposes panic buttons for Ohio's schools
item.description: Pressing the button three times signifies a medical emergency and pressing it eight or more times means there is an active shooter situation.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republican 'absolutely certain' there will be supplemental to address Helene aid
item.description: Rep. Kat Cammack (R-Fla.) said Monday she’s “absolutely certain” Congress will consider a supplemental aid package to provide additional disaster relief to areas devastated by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball’s all-time hits leader who endured a lifetime ban for betting on the sport, has died at age 83, his agent Jeff Lenkov confirmed to KTLA 5 News. According to TMZ, Rose died at his home in Las Vegas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Ex-University of Kentucky staffer asks if Hurricane Helene is act of God to punish MAGA supporters
item.description: A former staffer at the University of Kentucky floated the idea that God was using Hurricane Helene to punish Trump supporters in a social media post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams accepted harmless 'courtesies,' not bribes, his lawyer says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams launched a legal attack on the federal corruption case against him Monday, with his attorney asking a judge to toss out bribery charges and then holding a combative news conference accusing prosecutors of ethical lapses.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Inside NYPD's 'massive' security operation for UN General Assembly amid protests
item.description: The New York City Police Department implemented extensive security measures during the United Nations General Assembly in Midtown Manhattan, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: BioLab plant fire: More than 90K sheltering in place in Georgia as 'strong chemical smell' enters Atlanta area
item.description: Authorities in the Atlanta area are now reporting a "strong chemical smell" in the air following a chemical plant fire on Sunday in Conyers, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DirecTV buys rival Dish TV and Sling for just $1
item.description: DirecTV is buying Dish and Sling, a deal it has sought to complete for years, as the company seeks to better compete against streaming services that have become dominant.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, KRON4

item.title: Stuck NASA astronauts welcome SpaceX capsule that'll bring them home next year
item.description: The two astronauts stuck at the International Space Station since June welcomed their new ride home with Sunday’s arrival of a SpaceX capsule.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dad walks nearly 30 miles through flood debris to walk daughter down the aisle
item.description: What was supposed to be a two-hour drive for his daughter's wedding turned into seven hours by car and another five and a half hours on foot for David Jones.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah is one of the top 10 states where you're likely to find gold: survey
item.description: According to a survey conducted by SD Bullion, Colorado remains the seventh-highest gold-producing state in the United States even in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, ABC 4

item.title: Here's how CM Punk is helping WWE NXT's Roxanne Perez
item.description: CM Punk, in particular, has become a significant influence in her career, mentoring her through her rise in NXT.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, ABC 4

item.title: Supplies arrive by plane and mule as Helene death toll passes 100
item.description: Desperate residents isolated by washed-out roads and the lack of power and cellular service in western North Carolina lined up Monday for fresh water and a chance to message loved ones that they were alive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Single Utah mother, 33, raising money for her own funeral after cancer diagnosis gives her 3 months to live
item.description: A single Utah mother of two was told she has three months to live after she was diagnosed with terminal cancer and Cushing Syndrome, and is now planning her own funeral.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interstate is closed outside Atlanta as residents evacuate due to a chemical plant fire
item.description: CONYERS, Ga. (AP) — Some residents east of Atlanta were evacuated while others were told to shelter in place Sunday to avoid contaminants from a chemical plant fire that sent a massive plume of dark smoke high into the sky that could be seen from miles away.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California firefighters hurt in crash while heading to help Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: "These first responders put their own lives on the line every day, here at home and around the country when called," said San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, ABC 4

item.title: This Halloween decoration can be dangerous for deer, wildlife officials warn
item.description: Fall is in full swing and many have already decked out their properties with Halloween decorations, but wildlife experts say that one decoration in particular can be harmful to deer in the area.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Videos show severe flooding, damage from Helene
item.description: As the worst of Helene has moved on, many are taking to social media to share videos of the severe flooding and damage.
item.dc:creator: Mike Costanza, Fox 5

item.title: John Ashton of 'Beverly Hills Cop' dies at 76
item.description: In a career that spanned more than 50 years, Ashton was a regular face across TV series and films, including "Midnight Run,” “Little Big League” and “Gone Baby Gone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: Donald Trump tosses chicken tenders into crowd at college football game
item.description: Former President Donald Trump surprised college students on Saturday in Alabama with an unusual gift for football fans.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Price of Mega Millions tickets to go up: Here's how much
item.description: PIERRE, S.D. (KELO) -- Some big changes are coming soon for the Mega Millions lotto game, whether or not the South Dakota Lottery Commission or any other state that sells the tickets agrees with them.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mercer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Conservative Christians were skeptical of mail-in ballots. Now they are gathering them in churches
item.description: MENIFEE, California (AP) — With Election Day just a few weeks away, longtime church members Lucky Hartunian and Janie Booth sat outside the Revival Christian Fellowship's sanctuary in Menifee, California, inviting congregants to register to vote.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 64 dead and millions without power after Helene's deadly march across the Southeast
item.description: PERRY, Fla. (AP) — Massive rains from powerful Hurricane Helene left people stranded, without shelter and awaiting rescue Saturday, as the cleanup began from a tempest that killed at least 64 people, caused widespread destruction across the U.S. Southeast and left millions without power.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN SMITH, KATE PAYNE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump posts, then deletes, fake image of Kamala Harris with Diddy
item.description: The man in the real image taken in 2001 is talk show host Montel Williams, whom Harris dated at that time.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, WGN 9
